content:
Agent: Thank you for calling support, this is Priya, how can I help you today?
Rahul: Hey, yeah, so I emailed a couple days ago about this rate limiting thing, I haven't heard back and it's still happening, so I figured I'd just call.
Agent: Okay, I'm sorry about that. Can I get your account email so I can pull that up?
Rahul: Yeah it's rahul@acme.com
Agent: Okay give me one second... okay I see the ticket, this is regarding the 429 errors after your Pro upgrade?
Rahul: Yeah exactly. Like I upgraded specifically to fix this and it's still happening. We've got a client demo tomorrow and I really can't have this happening mid-demo, that would be really bad for us.
Agent: Totally understand. Let me just check a couple things on our end — can you tell me roughly what time today you saw the error?
Rahul: Uh, this morning around 10, and then again like an hour ago.
Agent: Okay. And is it happening on all your API calls or specific ones?
Rahul: Honestly I'm not a hundred percent sure, I'd have to check with my dev, he's the one who flagged it to me.
Agent: No worries, that's fine. So I'm seeing your account is correctly upgraded to Pro as of the 12th, so that part looks right on our side. I'm going to escalate this to our engineering team since it looks like it might be a limit that didn't get applied properly.
Rahul: Okay. I mean I appreciate that but like — this is the second time I'm explaining the whole thing now. Is there a ticket number or something so I don't have to repeat this again if it's still broken tomorrow?
Agent: Yeah of course, it's ticket 4872. And I've noted the demo deadline, I'll flag it as time-sensitive.
Rahul: Appreciate it. Thanks.
Agent: Of course, sorry again for the trouble. We'll be in touch.
